title: Rally DevGrid / ExtJS Grid — Feature Catalogue
topic: Rally DevGrid Sencha ExtJS grid component architecture — full feature catalogue

changelog.summary: Add Section 0: WorkItemsTree feature matrix, Vector-advantage list, implementation sequence, prop-set architecture.
Gap 1 closed: Rally rank fields documented as inferred (DragAndDropRank relative vs Rank absolute); Vector adopts DragAndDropRank-style relative API on fractional indexing.
Gap 2 closed: ExtJS 7 modern-toolkit delta documented; validates WorkItemsTree row-edit and widget-cell patterns over the deprecated classic-toolkit mental model.
Gap 3 closed: column-state persistence documented; Vector adopts two-layer model (local cookie + Save View). Matrix row #23 upgraded; Vector advantages list extended.

Gap 4 closed: Sencha Clipboard plugin surface + React ecosystem options surveyed; Vector decision = build useGridClipboard hook (not adopt rdsg/Wijmo/CSVBox); paste contract defined; matrix row #28 added; in-app cell clipboard advantage added.

Gap 5 closed: Rally/Sencha DnD plugin surface + TreeGrid public classes documented; Vector ranking semantics decided (DnD decoupled from sort); remaining plugin class names acknowledged internal-only; DnD-always-available advantage added.

summary: ExtJS Grid feature catalogue — columns, sort, group, inline edit, virtual scroll, locking, DnD — mapped to Vector Table.
content:
Contents
  1. 0. WorkItemsTree Feature Matrix & Roadmap
  2. 1. Overview
  3. 2. Column Types & Renderers
  4. 3. Sorting & Filtering
  5. 4. Grouping & Summaries
  6. 5. Selection & Inline Editing
  7. 6. Buffered Rendering & Virtual Scrolling
  8. 7. Column Locking & Pinning
  9. 8. Row Expander & Widget Columns
  10. 9. Drag-and-Drop & Reordering
  11. 10. Full Feature Catalogue
  12. 11. Rally DevGrid Specifics
  13. 12. Vector Applicability
  14. 13. Sources
  15. 14. Gaps

0. WorkItemsTree Feature Matrix & Roadmap v1.0.5

0.A — Intro

WorkItemsTree is the core component of the system. It is not a work-items-only widget; it is a configurable hierarchical primitive that any artefact type (Portfolio Items, Subscriptions, Flows, Topology nodes, etc.) can adopt by passing a prop config. The default preset = work items, execution-layer entry-point.

The component is structured as ResourceTree (generic primitive) wrapped by adopter presets such as WorkItemsTree and PortfolioItemsTree. Prop configurations live in the addressables registry (PLA-0005) under stable addresses like samantha.work-items._tree.workitems, so the config is discoverable by Samantha and lintable by the addressables linter.

Configuration is split into five prop sets: Set 1 — Data types, Set 2 — UI scaffold, Set 3 — Feature requirements, Set 4 — Cog menu, and Set 5 — Colour overrides. Detail in §0.E below.

Hard rule: what was previously framed as “deliberate deferrals” — cross-parent DnD reparent, bulk edit, saved views, tags/custom fields — are core requirements, not parking-lot items. They are numbered in the matrix below alongside everything else.

Legend

✅ done   🚧 partial   ❌ missing   📦 stubbed

0.B — Feature Matrix (ranked by importance & value)

#FeatureRally DevGridWorkItemsTreeGap & how to add (props / hooks / API)Vector advantage
1DnD rank within parentFloat-rank reorder, WSAPI PATCH (requires sort-by-Rank)❌ missing (parked in reference/dnd_tree.tsx)Wire useResourceRank + applyDrop + snapshot rollback; props { dnd: { strategy: "rank-within-parent", parentField: "parent_id" } }; API: PATCH /api/work-items/:id/rank { before_id?, after_id? }. Vector decouples DnD from sort — DnD always available; non-rank sort triggers a switch-to-Rank confirm.Server is order of truth; 250ms debounce; realtime echo via NOTIFY; DnD not gated by current sort
2Filter chips (Type / Status / Assignee)Always-visible filter bar📦 stubbed UI onlyReplace stub with controlled chip rows; props { filters: [{ kind: "type" }, { kind: "status" }, { kind: "assignee" }] }; API: append ?type=…&status=…&assignee=… to list callFilter state participates in URL deep-link (PLA-0018)
3Server-sort on every columnRemote sort via store proxy🚧 partial — only id sorts server-side; others are client-windowPromote sort to URL query param + server ORDER BY; props { sort: { mode: "server", default: "id:asc" } }; API: ?sort=<col>:<dir>Stable across pagination & realtime updates
4Keyboard nav (Tab/Enter cell traversal)Custom Rally Tab/Enter handler❌ missingAdd useKeyboardGrid hook on the table root: Tab → next editable cell in row; Enter → same column, next row; Esc cancels active editWorks with InlineEditField focus protocol already in place
5Multi-select (checkbox + Shift-range)checkboxmodel❌ missingAdd leading checkbox column when { selection: { mode: "multi" } }; expose selectedIds + onSelectionChangeSelection survives realtime row inserts via stable id keying
6Bulk-action toolbarFloating toolbar overlay❌ missingRender <BulkActionBar> when selectedIds.size > 0; actions wired to POST /api/work-items/bulk { ids, op, payload }Permission-gated via useHasPermission per action
7Sprint column (real)Real iteration field📦 FAKE — sprintAlias() derives S-NN from UUID hexResolve real sprint via existing sprints table join in list query; render via render: (r) => r.sprint?.alias ?? "—"—
8Owner column (real)Avatar + display name📦 FAKE — ownerGlyph() = 2-char monogram from UUIDJoin users on owner_id; render <OwnerChip user={r.owner} /> with avatar + nameRBAC-gated owner-picker (only show users you can assign to)
9Due date (real)Real due field📦 FAKE — dueLabel() = updated_at + 7 days; no schema columnAdd due_date DATE NULL column via migration; populate via inline date editor—
10Column visibility (show/hide picker)Header context menu + profile persist❌ missingAdd <ColumnPicker> flyout; persist visibility map to user_preferences table per { pageId, slot }Per-tenant defaults possible via addressables config
11Column locking (pin Title left)locked: true❌ missingAdd { key, locked: "left" } column flag; render two synced viewports with position: sticky on locked cellsCSS-only sticky (no dual-DOM-table sync overhead)
12Column reorder (drag header)Built-in header drag❌ missingWrap header row in @dnd-kit sortable; persist order to user_preferencesSame DnD library as row reorder — single mental model
13Multi-column sort (Shift-click)Sorters array❌ missingPromote sort to { field, dir }[]; URL: ?sort=field1:asc,field2:desc—
14Virtual scroll (buffered renderer)BufferedRenderer default-on❌ missing — full DOM renderAdopt @tanstack/react-virtual when row count > 500; props { virtual: { threshold: 500 } }Dynamic threshold per device class
15Row context menu (right-click)Context menu plugin❌ missingAdd onContextMenu handler showing the cog-menu items (§Set 4) at the cursorSame items as cog button — single source
16Tags / custom fieldsTags column + WSAPI custom fields❌ missing (CORE — not deferred)Add tags TEXT[] + custom_fields JSONB columns; render chip cell with autocomplete from workspace tag dictionaryJSONB enables per-tenant schemaless extension
17Group summaries (sum points per sprint)GroupingSummary❌ missingAdd { grouping: { by, summaries: [{ col, agg: "sum" }] } }; server returns one summary row per groupAggregation runs in Postgres, not browser
18Realtime updatesNone — manual refresh🚧 partial — adopters use useRefetchOnPush ad-hocBake into ResourceTree: useRefetchOnPush(rankTopic(resourceType, parentId)) driven by props.resourceTypeVector exclusive — multi-user live edits
19Permission-gated columns/actionsNone❌ missingPer-column / per-action { requires: "work-item.assign" } consumed via useHasPermissionVector exclusive — data-driven RBAC (PLA-0007)
20URL-encoded tree stateNone🚧 partial — open-set via PLA-0018Encode { openIds, sort, filters, selection, columnOrder } into search paramsVector exclusive — shareable deep-links to any tree state
21CSV export (server-side)Third-party plugin❌ missingGET /api/work-items.csv?…filters streamed from Postgres COPY TO STDOUTNo client memory cap; permission-checked at the route
22Cross-parent DnD reparentCross-grid drag with shared dragGroup❌ missing (CORE — not deferred)Drop on a different parent row → PATCH /api/work-items/:id { parent_id } + rank recomputeSingle tree (not cross-grid) — simpler hit-testing
23Saved views (two-layer)Saved View — captures filters/grouping/columns/scope❌ missing (CORE — not deferred)Layer A: in-memory + cookie for column visibility/order/sort/filter (always-on). Layer B: explicit "Save View" persists named view to user_view_state(user_id, address, name). URL state encodes Layer A so views are sharable ad-hoc without a DB row.Two-layer persistence — local-by-default + named profile views, addressable via Samantha; URL alone enough for ad-hoc share
24Keyboard new-item create (Enter on empty row)Custom Rally enhancement❌ missingSentinel last row when { canCreate: true }; Enter on empty → POST /api/work-itemsOptimistic insert with realtime echo dedup
25Copy URL to rowNone❌ missingCog-menu action: navigator.clipboard.writeText(rowDeepLink(r))Deep-link URL is canonical (PLA-0018)
26Mobile responsive collapseNone — fixed desktop layout❌ missingAt < 720px swap row template to card layout (Set 2 alt scaffold)Vector exclusive — same data, different scaffold per viewport
27Streaming skeleton via SuspenseNone — synchronous load❌ missingWrap fetch in <Suspense fallback={<TreeSkeleton/>}>; server-stream the first pageVector exclusive — Next.js App Router streaming
28Clipboard paste import (Excel/Sheets → tree)Ext.grid.plugin.Clipboard — formats only; no mapping/coercion❌ missingBuild useGridClipboard(refModel, onCommit) hook; ResourceTree onPaste prop; bulk endpoint POST /api/<resource>/bulk; see §14.4 for full contractIn-app cell clipboard preserves typed values cross-tree; cross-platform delimiter normalisation; configurable transactional vs partial-success

0.C — Features we can build that Rally never had

  • Realtime sync — every adopter inherits NOTIFY/refetch via useRefetchOnPush + rankTopic(); Rally was single-user-write.
  • RBAC columns & actions — useHasPermission(<code>) hides cells & cog items at render time; data-driven per PLA-0007.
  • Server-defined flow state machine — useWorkItemFlowStates returns transitions per type; UI never hard-codes status sets.
  • URL-encoded tree state — open-set, sort, filters, selection, column order all serialise into search params (PLA-0018); shareable deep-links to any view.
  • Streaming skeletons — Next.js Suspense lets the first page paint before later pages arrive; Rally blocked on full store load.
  • Mobile responsive — same component swaps row template (dense → card) below a breakpoint; Rally was desktop-only.
  • Samantha API addressability — every tree, column, and row is reachable as samantha.<page>._tree.<slot>.<id>; the bot can drive edits the user can’t reach with a mouse.
  • Server-side CSV export — COPY TO STDOUT streamed through the API route; no client-memory cap; permission-checked at the route.
  • Two-layer view persistence — local-by-default (in-memory + cookie) + explicit profile-saved named views (user_view_state), addressable via Samantha. Rally requires an explicit Save View; Vector keeps your column state across reloads automatically.
  • In-app cell clipboard — Sencha-style memory-only format keyed by (resourceType, rowSchema); cross-tree paste preserves typed values without re-coercion (no string-roundtrip via OS clipboard).
  • DnD always available — rank is structural, not column-bound. Rally's enableRanking requires the grid to be sorted by the Rank column to expose the drag handle; Vector exposes DnD regardless of current sort, with an opt-in "switch to Rank sort" confirm on drop.

0.D — Implementation sequence

Build order matches the §0.B ranking; later items depend on the foundations laid by earlier ones.

  1. Restore DnD rank from reference/dnd_tree.tsx into ResourceTree; wire useResourceRank + applyDrop + snapshot rollback.
  2. Replace stub filter chips with controlled rows in WorkItemsTree.tsx; thread state into URL.
  3. Promote sort to URL + server ORDER BY; remove client-window sort branches.
  4. Add useKeyboardGrid on the table root for Tab/Enter cell traversal.
  5. Add multi-select column behind { selection: { mode: "multi" } }; expose selectedIds.
  6. Build <BulkActionBar> + POST /api/work-items/bulk handler.
  7. Replace sprintAlias() FAKE with real sprints join.
  8. Replace ownerGlyph() FAKE with real users join + <OwnerChip>.
  9. Migration: add due_date DATE NULL to work_items; inline date editor.
  10. Build <ColumnPicker> persisted to user_preferences.
  11. Sticky-cell column locking; left-pin Title.
  12. Header DnD via @dnd-kit sortable; persist order.
  13. Multi-column sort (sorters array + URL).
  14. Virtual scroll with @tanstack/react-virtual at threshold 500.
  15. Row context menu reusing cog-menu items.
  16. Tags + custom fields: tags TEXT[] + custom_fields JSONB migration; chip cell.
  17. Group summaries: { grouping: { by, summaries } }; server aggregation.
  18. Bake useRefetchOnPush into ResourceTree (already partial in adopters).
  19. Permission-gated columns/actions throughout.
  20. Full URL-encoded tree state (extends PLA-0018).
  21. CSV export route via COPY TO STDOUT.
  22. Cross-parent DnD reparent: drop-on-row updates parent_id.
  23. Saved views: saved_views table + UI.
  24. Keyboard new-item create on Enter in sentinel row.
  25. Copy-URL cog action.
  26. Mobile responsive: card scaffold below 720px.
  27. Streaming skeleton via Suspense at the route boundary.

0.E — Prop-set architecture

SetControlsWorkItemsTree defaultPortfolioItemsTree example
Set 1 — Data types resourceType, entry-point layer, fetchers, parent FK field { resourceType: "work-item", entryLayer: "execution", fetcher: listWorkItems, parentField: "parent_id" } { resourceType: "portfolio-item", entryLayer: "strategy", fetcher: listPortfolioItems, parentField: "parent_layer_id" }
Set 2 — UI scaffold columns[], row template (dense | accordion | card), depth visualisation, header style { columns: [id, title, status, priority, points, sprint, owner], rowTemplate: "dense", depth: { kind: "tree-lines", step: 20 }, header: "compact" } { columns: [tag, name, layer, weight], rowTemplate: "accordion", depth: { kind: "indent", step: 24 }, header: "framed" }
Set 3 — Feature requirements inline edit, DnD, expand/collapse, multi-select, virtual scroll, keyboard nav, filter chips, server-sort { inlineEdit: true, dnd: { strategy: "rank-within-parent" }, expand: true, selection: { mode: "multi" }, virtual: { threshold: 500 }, keyboard: true, filters: [...], sort: { mode: "server" } } { inlineEdit: true, dnd: false, expand: true, selection: { mode: "single" }, virtual: false, keyboard: true, filters: [], sort: { mode: "client" } }
Set 4 — Cog menu per-row actions (edit, copy, delete, multi-edit, archive, custom); permission-gated [{ id: "edit", requires: "work-item.update" }, { id: "copy" }, { id: "archive", requires: "work-item.archive" }] [{ id: "edit", requires: "portfolio.update" }, { id: "split", custom: true }]
Set 5 — Colour overrides border, header bg, row bg, zebra y/n, selected/hover, type-badge palette inherits theme defaults { border: "#E2E8F0", headerBg: "#F8FAFC", zebra: true, selectedBg: "#EEF6FF", typeBadge: { strategic: "#6B46C1", tactical: "#3182CE" } }

0.F — Versioning

This document is a living, versioned spec. Current version: v1.0.0 (2026-05-06). The JSON envelope carries top-level version and changelog fields. Future updates bump version (semver) and prepend a changelog entry. Consumers (Dev → Research tab) re-read the JSON on each visit, so updates push themselves; a small renderer change can later surface the version pill under the title.

1. Overview

The ExtJS Grid (Sencha ExtJS) is the most feature-complete enterprise data-grid component in the pre-React era and remains a reference architecture for production grid design. Rally Software (now Broadcom ValueOps) built its entire work-item list, backlog, and planning views on top of ExtJS Grid — these are collectively known as the Rally DevGrid. This paper catalogues every major feature of ExtJS Grid 4.x/5.x/6.x and maps relevant patterns to Vector's <Table> component (PLA-0015), which aims to be the single sanctioned table primitive across the product.

The survey spans: column architecture, rendering pipeline, sorting, filtering, grouping, summaries, selection, inline editing, buffered/virtual scrolling, locked columns, row expanders, widget columns, drag-and-drop, and the Rally-specific customisations layered on top of the stock ExtJS Grid.

2. Column Types & Renderers

ExtJS Grid distinguishes two concepts: column types (which control layout and interaction) and cell renderers (which control what is drawn inside the cell). These map to Vector's columns prop on <Table>.

Built-in Column Xtype Catalogue

XtypeDescriptionKey Config
gridcolumnBase column — plain text render from record fielddataIndex, renderer, width, flex
numbercolumnRight-aligned numeric with format stringformat (e.g. '0,000.00')
datecolumnDate formatted via Ext.Date.formatformat (e.g. 'm/d/Y')
booleancolumnRenders true/false as configurable stringstrueText, falseText, undefinedText
templatecolumnCell rendered by an XTemplate stringtpl
actioncolumnOne or more icon buttons per row, no field bindingitems[] with icon, tooltip, handler
checkcolumnCheckbox per row; toggles a boolean field on clickdataIndex, editable
rownumbererAuto-incrementing row number; not bound to a fieldrowspan, width
widgetcolumnEmbeds any Ext widget (button, sparkline, progress) per cellwidget config object
Ext.grid.column.Column (locked)Variant pinned left/rightlocked: true, lockable: false

Renderer Function Contract

Every column's renderer(value, metaData, record, rowIndex, colIndex, store, view) receives the full record and can return any HTML string. This is the most flexible extension point — Rally DevGrid uses it extensively to render coloured state tags, owner avatars, and priority icons directly inside cells without custom column xtypes.

ExtJS 6 modernised this with cell (a config that binds the cell to a widget instance) and kept renderer for string-returning functions. Vector's columns[].render prop is the direct equivalent.

3. Sorting & Filtering

Sorting

ExtJS Grid sorting is driven by the bound Ext.data.Store. There are two modes:

  • Local sort — the store sorts its in-memory record array using a comparator. Multi-column sort is supported; each column contributes a { property, direction } sorter.
  • Remote sort — the store emits a proxy request with sort params; the server returns pre-sorted data. Sort state is maintained client-side but data fetching is delegated.

Header click toggles ASC → DESC → no-sort for that column. Holding Shift adds the column to the existing sort set (multi-sort). The column header renders a sort indicator arrow. sortable: false disables sort for individual columns.

Filtering

The Ext.grid.filters.Filters plugin adds column-level filter UI. Each column type gets an appropriate filter control:

Column typeFilter widget
StringText input (contains / starts-with / ends-with)
NumberGreater than / Less than pair of number inputs
DateBefore / After / On date pickers
ListCheckbox list of distinct values
BooleanYes / No radio

Filters can be local (applied to store data) or remote (serialised into the proxy request). The filter bar appears as a second row under the column headers or as a per-column dropdown triggered from the header menu. Rally DevGrid used the header-menu variant with custom filter types for owner (multi-user picker) and tag (tag autocomplete).

4. Grouping & Summaries

Grouping

The Ext.grid.feature.Grouping feature groups rows by a store grouper field. Visually this renders a collapsible group header row above each group of records. Key behaviours:

  • Groups collapse/expand individually. startCollapsed: true collapses all groups on load.
  • Group headers are rendered via groupHeaderTpl — an XTemplate that receives the group field value, count, and all records in the group.
  • Multiple groupers are supported (group by sprint, then by assignee, for example), producing nested group headers.
  • Drag-and-drop within groups can be configured so items dragged between groups update the grouper field automatically.

Summary Row

The Ext.grid.feature.Summary feature appends a summary row at the bottom of the grid (or per-group with the GroupingSummary feature variant). Each column declares its summary type:

Summary typeBehaviour
sumNumeric sum of all rows in the group
countNumber of records in the group
minMinimum value
maxMaximum value
averageArithmetic mean
functionCustom aggregator function

The GroupingSummary feature combines both — one summary row per group plus an optional grand-total footer row. Rally DevGrid used per-group summaries to show story point totals per sprint and defect count per owner block.

5. Selection & Inline Editing

Selection Models

Selection modelBehaviour
rowmodelClick selects the entire row. Ctrl/Shift for multi-select. Default.
cellmodelClick selects an individual cell. Arrow keys navigate between cells. Required by the cell editing plugin.
spreadsheetmodelExtJS 6+ — Excel-like rectangular range selection with copy-to-clipboard support.
checkboxmodelAdds a leading checkbox column; selected rows are checked. Supports select-all header checkbox.

Editing Plugins

ExtJS Grid provides two editing plugins, both requiring cellmodel or rowmodel selection:

  • CellEditing (Ext.grid.plugin.CellEditing) — double-click (or single-click with clicksToEdit: 1) activates an in-cell editor widget. Each column declares its editor config (textfield, datepicker, combobox, etc.). Tab/Enter confirms; Escape cancels. Confirmation writes directly to the record.
  • RowEditing (Ext.grid.plugin.RowEditing) — clicking a row activates an editor bar inline under the row header for all editable columns simultaneously. Provides Update / Cancel buttons. Good for multi-field validation before commit.

Rally DevGrid used CellEditing with custom editor widgets: owner-picker (user search autocomplete), date-range picker (start + end in one editor), and state-dropdown (custom combobox with colour swatches). These map to Vector's inline-edit slot on <Table> columns.

6. Buffered Rendering & Virtual Scrolling

For datasets exceeding a few hundred rows, ExtJS Grid's Buffered Renderer plugin (Ext.grid.plugin.BufferedRenderer, enabled by default in ExtJS 5+) renders only the rows visible in the viewport plus a configurable buffer zone above and below. This is DOM-level virtualisation — only visible rows exist in the DOM at any time.

Key parameters

ConfigDefaultEffect
trailingBufferZone20Rows rendered below the visible area
leadingBufferZone20Rows rendered above the visible area
numFromEdge2Rows from viewport edge before a repaint is triggered
variableRowHeightfalseEnable for expanded rows or rows with dynamic content height

The companion to the buffered renderer is the Buffered Store (buffered: true on Ext.data.Store), which pages data from the server transparently. As the user scrolls, the store fetches the next page automatically. Together, these two features allow Rally DevGrid to display 10,000+ backlog items without loading all records into the browser.

ExtJS does not support fixed-height-only rows by default when variableRowHeight is false — row heights are measured on first paint and cached. Rally DevGrid enforced fixed row heights via CSS (line-height clamps) to keep virtualisation predictable.

7. Column Locking & Pinning

The Ext.grid.plugin.LockingView (enabled via enableLocking: true on the grid or locked: true on individual columns) splits the grid into two synchronised sub-grids:

  • Locked section — left-side, fixed position, scrolls only vertically. Contains columns with locked: true.
  • Normal section — right-side, scrolls horizontally and vertically in sync with the locked section.

Users can drag columns between sections at runtime (if lockable: true on the column, which is the default when locking is enabled). The header context menu gains Lock / Unlock items. Locked columns always render on the left; right-side pinning requires a custom approach (ExtJS 6 introduced rightLocked).

Rally DevGrid locked the Name/Title column by default so users could scroll the date, estimate, and status columns without losing the item name. This is the canonical use case for column locking in PM tools.

Performance note: The locked/normal split creates two separate DOM tables. Scroll sync is achieved via JavaScript. On very wide grids with many locked columns the sync overhead is measurable. ExtJS 6 rewrote locking using CSS transforms for smoother performance.

8. Row Expander & Widget Columns

Row Expander

The Ext.grid.plugin.RowExpander plugin adds a toggle button (chevron) in a leading column. Clicking it expands the row to show additional content rendered by an XTemplate or a full Ext component. Key config:

  • rowBodyTpl — XTemplate string rendered as static HTML below the row cells.
  • expandOnDblClick: true — double-clicking anywhere on the row toggles expansion.
  • Expanded content can contain nested grids (tree-in-grid patterns), charts, or detail panels.
  • Requires variableRowHeight: true on the buffered renderer when virtual scrolling is active.

Rally DevGrid used row expanders on the Portfolio Items grid to show child stories inline. Vector's <Table> has an analogous expander prop defined in PLA-0015.

Widget Column

The Ext.grid.column.Widget xtype (ExtJS 5.1+) embeds an Ext widget instance per cell, recycling widget instances as rows scroll in and out of the viewport (a process called widget attach/detach). Supported widget types include progress bars, sparklines (via third-party), sliders, buttons, and any custom Ext.Widget subclass. This is the correct mechanism when a cell needs interactive, state-aware UI rather than a static renderer string.

Vector's equivalent is the render prop on a column which can return a React element. Since React reconciles the entire tree, the recycling concern is handled automatically by React's virtual DOM — no manual attach/detach needed.

9. Drag-and-Drop & Reordering

Row Drag-and-Drop (Reordering within a grid)

The Ext.grid.plugin.DragDrop plugin (used with Ext.grid.plugin.GridDragDrop in ExtJS 6) enables dragging rows within and between grids. Configuration:

  • dragGroup / dropGroup — named groups that control which grids accept drops from which drag sources.
  • copy: true — drag copies the record instead of moving it.
  • Drop position is determined by the drop target's validDrop method, which receives the drag source, target record, and position ('before' | 'after').

Column Reordering

Column header drag-and-drop is built into ExtJS Grid with no plugin required. Users drag column headers to reorder. draggable: false locks a column in place. Column order is persisted to state providers (LocalStorage or server) via the stateful config.

Column Resizing

Users drag the column edge to resize. resizable: false prevents resizing for a column. Column widths are also persisted via the state provider. minWidth and maxWidth enforce bounds.

Rally DevGrid used row drag-and-drop to reorder backlog items (updating the Rank field on the story). Cross-grid drag was used in the drag-to-sprint feature — dragging a story from the backlog grid into a sprint grid updated the iteration field. Vector's DnD convention uses @dnd-kit for the same patterns.

10. Full Feature Catalogue

Nested grid (tree-in-grid)RowExpander with child gridExpander + nested <Table>
Coloured row CSSgetRowClass(record)rowClassName prop

Empty textemptyText configemptyState prop
Load maskAutomatic from store load eventsloading prop

11. Rally DevGrid Specifics

Rally DevGrid is not a public library — it is the internal name for the suite of ExtJS Grid customisations that Rally (Broadcom) built on top of the stock grid for its PM surfaces: the backlog, iteration plan, portfolio kanban, and custom list views. The known features are inferred from Rally's public documentation, the Broadcom TechDocs, and community references:

Custom Column Types Used in Rally

ColumnBehaviour
Name/Title columnLocked left; renders item type icon + hyperlink to detail page; inline-edit on double-click triggers a text field
State columnColoured pill renderer; click opens a state dropdown combobox; state transitions are validated server-side
Owner columnAvatar + display name renderer; click opens a user-search autocomplete; supports unassigned state
Estimate (Story Points)Numeric cell; inline edit with integer validation; 0 renders as empty to reduce noise
Iteration columnSprint name renderer; drag-target column — dropping a row onto a different iteration row updates the iteration field
Tags columnMulti-tag chip renderer; click opens tag picker; tags are workspace-scoped
Rank columnHidden by default; drives row sort order; updated via drag-and-drop; stores a floating-point rank value (WS-DEC or Fibonacci basis depending on version)
Blocked columnRed icon renderer when Blocked: true; click opens a blocker reason textarea editor
Ready columnCheckbox column; toggling marks item as ready-for-sprint-pull
Expedite columnBoolean; expedited items render with a highlighted row class

Rally Grid Behaviours

  • Rank drag-and-drop: Rows are draggable within the backlog. Drop position sends a WSAPI PATCH with the new rank value (a decimal between the rank of the rows above and below the drop target — the Fibonacci ranking algorithm).
  • Inline edit on Enter/Tab: Confirming a cell edit moves focus to the next editable cell in the same column (Tab) or the same column in the next row (Enter). This enables rapid keyboard data entry — a Rally-specific UX enhancement over stock ExtJS behaviour.
  • Bulk edit toolbar: Selecting multiple rows shows a floating toolbar with bulk-edit operations (move to iteration, change owner, change state). This is an overlay pattern on top of the grid, not an ExtJS feature.
  • Copy/paste from spreadsheet: Rally DevGrid (post-2014) allowed pasting clipboard content into the grid, with column-mapping inference. Used for importing backlog items from Excel.
  • Column picker: A UI flyout listing all available columns with show/hide checkboxes. Column visibility is persisted to the user's profile (server-side state, not localStorage).
  • Filter bar: A persistent second row of filter inputs above the data rows. Rally chose the always-visible filter bar (not the dropdown-per-column) because PM users frequently filter by owner, state, or iteration.

12. Vector Applicability

Vector's <Table> component (PLA-0015) is the single sanctioned table primitive. This section maps Rally DevGrid's feature catalogue to the current implementation and identifies gaps worth tracking.

Rally/ExtJS featureVector statusPriority signal

Column show/hide pickerNot yet in <Table>Medium — user preference feature
Column reordering (drag header)Not yetMedium — power-user feature
Column locking (pin left)Not yetMedium — needed once tables are wide
Virtual scrolling (buffered render)Not yet — full DOMHigh — required at 500+ rows

Bulk action toolbarNot yet (would sit above <Table>)Medium

Action columnactions on columnDone

Key Architectural Lesson

Rally's biggest grid UX investment was in contextual inline editing — making it feel natural to edit data directly in the list rather than opening a separate form. The friction reduction from double-click-to-edit vs click-to-open-drawer is significant for high-frequency data operations (story point entry, state transitions, owner assignment). Vector's inlineEdit prop on <Table> is the right mechanism; ensuring it covers the PM-critical columns (state, owner, estimate) is the most impactful short-term investment.

14. Gaps — closed (v1.0.5)

The five original gaps have been worked through with user-sourced research. Each closure produced architectural decisions for Vector; those land in §0 as well as here.

14.1 — Rally rank field payload researched

Rally exposes two rank surfaces: DragAndDropRank (relative — target Artifact + before/after position) and Rank (absolute lexicographic string). Wire payload shapes are not publicly documented; the above is inferred from SDK behaviour and forum threads. Vector decision: adopt a DragAndDropRank-style relative API on top of fractional indexing — clients send { before_id?, after_id? }, server computes fractional rank.

14.2 — ExtJS 7 modern-toolkit grid delta closed

Modern toolkit's grid is structurally different from classic. Highlights confirmed from Sencha docs:

  • Ext.grid.Grid extends Ext.dataview.List (not Ext.panel.Panel like classic).
  • rowedit plugin replaces classic CellEditing / RowEditing.
  • Virtual Store replaces buffered store for large datasets.
  • selectable config replaces classic selection-model classes.
  • Multi-grouping and grid summary added in 7.4.
  • Column state stateful in 7.6.

Validation for Vector: WorkItemsTree's widget-cell + transactional row-edit pattern aligns with the modern toolkit, not the classic one. We are not chasing a deprecated mental model.

14.3 — Column-state persistence Vector decision

Rally's Saved View captures filters / grouping / displayed fields / columns / items-per-page / scoping. The low-level persistence mechanism (server profile vs LocalStorage) is not publicly documented. Vector decision (two-layer):

  • Layer A — Local view state. In-memory + cookie. Always-on. Persists column visibility / order / sort / filter across reload on the same machine. No user action required.
  • Layer B — Saved Views. Named, profile-stored. Explicit "Save View" action writes to user_view_state keyed by (user_id, address, name). Sharable across the user's machines; addressable via Samantha.

This upgrades matrix row #23 (§0.B) and adds a Vector advantage to §0.C.

14.4 — Clipboard-paste / import Vector contract

Sencha's Ext.grid.plugin.Clipboard (Modern 8.0) defines four formats — cell (model-aware, memory-only), text (HTML-stripped), html (rendered), raw (dataIndex values) — and four configs (system, source, formats, memory). The cell format is excluded from the system clipboard (memory-only, same-app paste, model-aware). Methods: getTextData(format, erase) / putTextData(data, format). The plugin is silent on column-mapping, row-create vs overwrite, type coercion, validation, and bulk-API — those are consumer concerns.

Build-vs-buy for Vector. React ecosystem options surveyed: react-datasheet-grid (MIT, Excel paste built-in but parallel rendering stack), Wijmo FlexGrid (commercial), CSVBox (modal import widget — not in-place). Adopting any of these would split ResourceTree's DnD/RBAC/Samantha stack. Decision: build.

Vector paste contract (the rules ResourceTree wires up):

  • Mapping: exact header (case-insensitive) → positional fallback → unmatched-column confirmation dialog.
  • Row mode: paste from cursor down; create new rows on overflow; empty cells leave field at default.
  • Type coercion: model-driven (per ResourceTree Set 1); validate-then-commit.
  • Conflict: silent overwrite within selection range; append below selection if paste exceeds it.
  • Endpoint: POST /api/<resource>/bulk, transactional with optional partial-success report.
  • Cross-platform: normalise \r\n/\r/\n to \n; sniff TSV → CSV → HTML-table; strip Windows trailing-newline trailer row.
  • In-app cell equivalent: Vector ships an in-memory clipboard keyed by (resourceType, rowSchema) for cross-tree paste preserving typed values — mirrors Sencha's cell.

Implementation lives in a small useGridClipboard(refModel, onCommit) hook reusable by ResourceTree, <Table>, and any future grid.

14.5 — Rally plugin class names mostly internal

Confirmed public:

  • Rally.ui.grid.TreeGrid and Rally.ui.grid.TreeGridView — unminified SDK source published at help.rallydev.com/apps/2.1/doc/source/TreeGrid.html; xtype aliased via 'widget.mythingy' pattern.
  • Community extension @agile-central-technical-services/utils-treegrid-derived-columns demonstrates "derived columns" — columns outside the data model. Vector parity already exists: ResourceTree columns prop accepts a cell(row) render fn — no schema column required.
  • Rally's drag-to-rank is a single config enableRanking: true on rallygrid with a hard precondition: the grid must be sorted by the Rank column for the six-dot drag handle to appear. Rally wraps Sencha's Ext.grid.plugin.RowDragDrop (configs copy/dragText/dragIcon; events beforedrop/drop; built on Ext.drag.Source/Target). Classic toolkit equivalent was Ext.grid.plugin.DragDrop (alias gridviewdragdrop). Ext.dataview.plugin.SortableList is not in the documented Modern surface.

Vector ranking semantics: rank is structural, not column-bound. DnD is always available regardless of current sort. When the user has applied a non-rank sort, dropping a row prompts: "Reordering will switch sort to Rank — proceed?" (default; per-adopter override). Drop event shape {records, target, position: 'before'|'after'} mirrors RowDragDrop so the implementation can swap without API churn.

Closed as internal-only (not in public Rally SDK; no further research planned): editing plugins (inline-add, bulk-edit), column locking / reorder / visibility, selection model (Modern's selectable is the architectural reference for Vector regardless), buffered/virtual scroll, filter chip bar, paste plugin, bulk-action toolbar. Vector takes Sencha Modern as the architectural reference where Rally is opaque, and our own decisions where Modern doesn't prescribe.
